messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS. Request: create hello world app, where I can modify the text \"hello world\" colors through a frontend UI.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "initialize-project",
      "title": "Initialize project and install dependencies",
      "description": "Create a new Node project, then install Express, Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, and Autoprefixer. Ensure a clean folder structure for server and static assets.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "package.json exists and npm install completes with express, tailwindcss, postcss, and autoprefixer listed as dependencies or devDependencies."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind-build",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build pipeline",
      "description": "Initialize Tailwind with PostCSS, set proper content paths, create input CSS with @tailwind directives, and add npm scripts for building and watching CSS output to public/styles.css.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "initialize-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Running npm run build:css generates public/styles.css containing Tailwind utilities without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-express-server",
      "title": "Implement Express server and static hosting",
      "description": "Create server.js to serve static files from a public directory and respond to the root path with index.html. Add an npm start script to launch the server.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "initialize-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "npm start launches an Express server that serves files from /public and returns index.html at GET /."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Build frontend page with color controls",
      "description": "Create public/index.html using Tailwind for layout and styles. Add a visible 'Hello World' element and UI controls (e.g., color input and preset buttons). Add public/app.js to update the text color on user interaction.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind-build",
        "implement-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Loading the page shows a styled 'Hello World' and controls; changing a control updates the text color immediately without reload."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-run-and-verify",
      "title": "Build assets, run server, and verify UI behavior",
      "description": "Execute the Tailwind build, start the Express server, and test the UI in a browser to confirm that color changes apply and no console errors occur.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "build-frontend-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "public/styles.css is generated, server runs on the chosen port, and the 'Hello World' color changes via UI controls in the browser."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 5,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down setup: initialize dependencies, configure Tailwind build, stand up Express static server, implement UI controls, then verify end-to-end."
}
